Name: Speed is everything; I need to process 100 applications in an hour.
If I have to click three times just to see a phone number, the UI has failed me.
Our historical candidate data is a complete mess.
Traditional ATS parsing rejects highly qualified candidates.
Efficiency: "How can I filter out the unqualified people instantly so I can focus on the real contenders
Collaboration: "I hope the hiring manager actually reads my notes this time."
Data Integrity: "If recruiters keep moving too fast, this database will be useless in six months."
Candidate Experience: "Are we losing good talent because our application process is too clunky?"
Relies heavily on the "Candidates List" view for day-to-day operations.
Scans candidate profiles using the "six-second rule" before making a decision.
Frequently uses bulk actions (bulk reject, bulk advance) to manage high volume.
Filters actively by strict dealbreakers (location, visa) and specific skill keywords.
Shares profiles via email when the system lacks native @tagging features.
Frustrated by "click fatigue" and forced PDF downloads just to view a resume.
Overwhelmed when faced with massive, unfiltered candidate lists and messy historical data.
Relieved when a profile is clean, scannable, and presents all necessary context in one view.
Anxious about missing out on top talent due to rigid system constraints.
